tasks.title: Project Setup and Directory Structure
tasks.description: Initialize Python 3.11 project with uv/pip, create directory layout, requirements.txt, and basic config.py with paths and presets registry.
tasks.details: Use `uv init glitchframe`; create folders: pipeline/, presets/, assets/shaders/, assets/fonts/, cache/, outputs/; generate requirements.txt with gradio==4.x, torch==2.x (cuda12), diffusers, librosa, soundfile, moderngl, skia-python, pillow, numpy; config.py with default paths, preset dict loading YAMLs.
tasks.testStrategy: Verify dir structure exists, `uv sync` installs without errors, `python config.py` loads without ImportError.
tasks.expansionPrompt: No expansion needed; straightforward setup.

tasks.title: Gradio UI Skeleton Implementation

tasks.testStrategy: Launch `python app.py`, verify all UI elements render, uploads/inputs accept data, buttons clickable without crash.

tasks.title: Audio Loading and Waveform Preview

tasks.details: Use soundfile/librosa to load MP3/WAV/FLAC; resample to 44100Hz mono; generate numpy waveform; Gradio Audio component for preview, custom JS for scrub if needed; hash song for cache dir.
tasks.testStrategy: Upload sample audio, verify waveform displays, play/scrub works, cache/<hash>/original.wav saved.
tasks.expansionPrompt: Split into: 1) Audio loading, resampling, and caching with hashing; 2) Waveform generation and Gradio Audio component integration with preview/playback.

tasks.title: Audio Analyzer with BeatNet and Features
tasks.description: Implement Analyze button: tempo/beats (BeatNet/madmom), onsets (librosa), spectral bands (8 log-mel), RMS, structural segments; cache analysis.json.
tasks.details: pipeline/audio_analyzer.py: load vocals via demucs('htdemucs_ft'); BeatNet for beats; librosa.onset.onset_strength.peaks; librosa.feature.melspectrogram(8 bands); librosa.rms; librosa.segment; save json with beats/onsets/spectrum/rms/segments.
tasks.testStrategy: Run Analyze on sample, verify analysis.json fields populated accurately vs librosa docs, BPM matches known tracks.
tasks.expansionPrompt: Divide into: 1) Demucs vocal separation; 2) Beat/tempo detection with BeatNet/madmom; 3) Onset, spectral, RMS feature extraction with librosa; 4) Structural segmentation and JSON caching.

tasks.title: Orchestrator Core with Song Hashing and Caching
tasks.description: Create orchestrator.py to coordinate pipeline stages, compute song_hash from audio+metadata, manage cache/<hash>/, skip re-analysis if exists.
tasks.details: Hash = hashlib.sha256(audio_bytes + metadata_str); mkdir cache/<hash>; load/save analysis.json/stems; orchestrate stages sequentially with progress callbacks to Gradio.
tasks.testStrategy: Simulate re-run with existing cache, verify skips analysis, progress updates correctly.
tasks.expansionPrompt: Break down to: 1) Implement song hashing from audio/metadata; 2) Cache directory management and JSON/stems persistence; 3) Sequential pipeline orchestration with Gradio progress callbacks and skip logic.

tasks.title: Preset System and YAML Loading

tasks.title: Simple Spectrum Visualizer Renderer (M1)
tasks.description: For M1: render plain 1080p video with solid color bg + spectrum overlay from analysis, pipe raw BGR to ffmpeg NVENC, mux audio.
tasks.details: pipeline/renderer.py: loop over frames at 30fps, interpolate spectrum[frame], draw bars with numpy/Pillow; ffmpeg cmd as spec'd: rawvideo bgr24 stdin -> h264_nvenc cq19; test end-to-end.
tasks.testStrategy: Render full song, ffprobe output.mp4: 1080p30, no A/V drift >50ms, spectrum syncs to beats.
tasks.expansionPrompt: Decompose into: 1) Frame loop with spectrum interpolation; 2) Numpy/Pillow bar drawing on solid BG; 3) Raw BGR piping to ffmpeg NVENC; 4) Audio muxing and A/V sync validation.

tasks.title: Moderngl Reactive Shader Layer
tasks.description: Implement GPU shader pass with moderngl offscreen FBO for reactive overlays driven by beat_phase, band_energies[8], rms, onset_pulse.

tasks.title: Frame Compositor Pipeline

tasks.title: Scope Preview to 10s Window with Background Optimization
tasks.description: Modify orchestrator to pass preview time range to BackgroundSource generators (SDXL, AnimateDiff, Ken Burns) for partial asset generation only within loudest 10s + padding; ensure full render completes missing assets with proper cache keys for unchanged quality.
tasks.details:
1. In orchestrator.py (from Task 5), add preview_start/end params from Task 18 loudest_10s analysis: def ensure_backgrounds(hash, preview_range=None, full_duration=None): if preview_range: t_start, t_end = preview_range; padding=1.0s; gen_range=(max(0,t_start-padding), min(full_duration, t_end+padding)) else: gen_range=(0,full_duration)
2. Update BackgroundSource.ensure(mode, song_hash, time_range=gen_range): cache_key=f'{hash}_{mode}_{gen_range[0]:.1f}_{gen_range[1]:.1f}' to distinguish partial/full caches in cache/<hash>/backgrounds/
3. Modify Task14 SDXL: pipe(prompts_for_range=[section_desc for t in np.arange(gen_range[0],gen_range[1],8)]).images → keyframe_{t:.1f}.png
4. Task15 AnimateDiff/KenBurns: generate loops/zooms only for gen_range, save with time-specific keys
5. Full render: call ensure_backgrounds(hash, full_duration=analysis['duration']) to fill gaps, using existing partial cache
6. Update docs/preview-and-render.md: explain partial caching; background-modes.md: time-range param; Gradio: 'Preview generates backgrounds for 10s window only (~2min vs 25min)'
7. Cache manifest.json: track generated ranges per mode for resume logic.
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Preview 30s song: verify BackgroundSource cache keys contain 'X.X_Y.Y' (10s range), SDXL generates ~2 keyframes (<30s total), preview completes <3min
2. Full render same song: new cache keys for full range, uses partial cache (no re-gen for preview range), ffprobe full video complete/quality matches non-preview baseline
3. Check cache/<hash>/backgrounds/: partial files from preview, complete after full render; manifest.json lists ranges
4. Gradio: progress shows 'Backgrounds (10s preview)' vs 'Backgrounds (full 180s)'; docs updated verifiable
5. Edge: preview at track edge → padding clamps 0/duration; no A/V issues ffprobe drift<50ms.
tasks.expansionPrompt: Split into: 1) Orchestrator preview_range param + gen_range logic with padding; 2) BackgroundSource time-range cache keys and manifest tracking; 3) Update SDXL/AnimateDiff/Ken Burns for partial generation with time-specific keys; 4) Full render gap-filling + cache reuse logic; 5) Gradio progress updates, docs, and edge case handling.

tasks.title: Bass-Driven Logo Beat Pulse with Low-Frequency Energy
tasks.description: Replace generic beat pulse with bass/kick-driven animation using low-frequency energy from analysis.json; add tunable sensitivity in CompositorConfig and preserve optional 'every beat' mode.
tasks.details:
**Core Changes in compositor/beat_pulse.py:**
1. Load analysis.json: extract **low-freq energy** - use `spectrum_bands[0:2]` (bass ~20-250Hz, 8 log-mel bands from Task 4) or compute `librosa.feature.rms(y=librosa.stft(analysis['audio'], n_fft=2048)[:,:,:2])` for sub-bass.
2. **Bass envelope generator**: `def bass_envelope(analysis, frame_time, sensitivity=1.0): bass_energy = np.mean(spectrum_bands[frame_time*sr//hop:frame_time*sr//hop+frame_len, 0:2]); return np.clip(bass_energy*sensitivity, 0, 1)`
3. **Alternative signals**: kick-detect = `onset_strength[beats] > threshold` filtered to bass band, or `librosa.onset.onset_strength(analysis['audio'].lowpass(250))` peaks.
4. **Logo params**: `scale = 1.0 + 0.3*bass_envelope; opacity = 0.7 + 0.3*bass_envelope` (smooth with `scipy.signal.sosfilt` 0.1s attack/decay).
5. **Config**: In `CompositorConfig`: `pulse_mode='bass' | 'beats' | 'hybrid'; bass_sensitivity=Slider(0.1-3.0, default=1.0)`; Gradio slider in UI.
6. **Fallback**: If `pulse_mode='beats'`, use existing `beat_grid` interpolation.
7. **Docs**: Update `title-and-beat-pulse.md`: explain bass vs beats modes, sensitivity tuning, freq ranges used.
**Extend audio_analyzer.py if needed**: Add `bass_rms = librosa.rms(librosa.stft(audio)[:,:,:2])` to analysis.json.
**Cache**: `pulse_cache/{song_hash}_{mode}_{sensitivity:.1f}.npy` for envelope per frame.
tasks.testStrategy:
1. **Analysis verify**: `python -m pipeline.audio_analyzer sample_bassheavy.mp3` → check `analysis.json` has `spectrum_bands.shape[1]>=2`, `bass_rms` if added.
2. **Pulse render test**: Mock 10s analysis slice → render logo-only frames, verify **scale/opacity** peaks align with kick hits (visualize matplotlib subplot(energy, pulse)) not every hi-hat.
3. **UI test**: Gradio → Analyze bass-heavy track (e.g. EDM), slider bass_sensitivity=0.5/1.0/2.0 → Preview: logo **pulsing cleanly on kicks** (no jitter on snares), mode='beats' falls back to grid.
4. **Full render**: EDM track → ffprobe video, extract logo frames → **energy correlation**: `np.corrcoef(bass_rms[::30], logo_scales)>0.7`; weak-beat track (acoustic) → minimal/no jitter.
5. **Docs**: Verify `title-and-beat-pulse.md` explains tuning/modes.
tasks.expansionPrompt: Split into: 1) Extend audio_analyzer for bass RMS/envelope extraction to analysis.json; 2) Implement beat_pulse.py bass mode with smoothing and fallback to beats; 3) Add CompositorConfig pulse_mode/bass_sensitivity + Gradio UI sliders; 4) Cache implementation + docs for tuning.

tasks.title: Overnight / multi-song render queue
tasks.description: Allow queuing multiple full-pipeline jobs (ingest + analysis + background + compositor) so batches can run unattended, with stable file paths for uploads and clear per-job success/failure handling.
tasks.details: Define a durable job spec (serialize OrchestratorInputs or equivalent: song_hash or audio path, background mode, preset, lyrics toggle/text, resolution/fps, metadata, paths to copied logo/static assets). Prefer phased delivery: (A) CLI or script that reads JSON/JSONL and calls orchestrate_full_render sequentially; (B) optional Gradio: Add to queue, list/clear, background worker thread or subprocess so the UI stays responsive. Copy Gradio temp uploads into per-job folders when enqueueing. Between jobs, rely on existing GPU memory teardown patterns. Document in docs/technical/ or handover: queue file format, overnight usage.
tasks.testStrategy: Enqueue2+ jobs with different hashes; verify outputs land in distinct outputs/<run_id>/; failure in job 2 does not corrupt job 1 artifacts; optional: restart mid-queue resumes from remaining entries if persistence implemented.
tasks.expansionPrompt: Split into: 1) Define durable job spec schema + JSON/JSONL serialization from OrchestratorInputs; 2) CLI batch runner with sequential orchestrate_full_render and error isolation; 3) Per-job asset copying + stable output paths; 4) Gradio queue UI with background worker/threading.

tasks.title: Single primary export action in Gradio (full pipeline UX)
tasks.description: Reduce perceived multi-step friction: one clear control that runs the same chain as Render full video today (analysis + optional lyrics alignment + background ensure + compositor + metadata), with advanced actions still available.
tasks.details: orchestrate_full_render already chains stages; the gap is UX/documentation. Add a prominent Export / Run full pipeline button (or rename and group) that uses the same code path as btn_render. Clarify in UI copy that Analyze / Align are optional precache/debug; when typography is enabled, render includes alignment if lyrics text present. Update docs/technical/gradio-ui.md and preview-and-render.md to describe the one-button story. Do not change stage order in orchestrator unless product requires it.
tasks.testStrategy: From cold state: ingest audio, fill metadata/preset, single export click produces output.mp4 + metadata.txt without requiring separate Analyze/Align clicks when lyrics+typography on.
tasks.expansionPrompt: Split into: 1) Add prominent Gradio Export button wired to orchestrate_full_render with conditional lyrics alignment; 2) Update UI copy/docs to emphasize one-button flow vs optional precache steps.

tasks.title: Timestamped section headers for lyrics alignment
tasks.description: Allow the user to mark section boundaries with a single line that carries both a human-readable label (Verse 1, Chorus, Pre-chorus, Bridge, Outro, ...) AND a [m:ss] timestamp. The header produces both a section break (equivalent to today's `---`) and an immovable line anchor on the first lyric line of that section, eliminating the need to stack `---` with an inline `[m:ss]` on every section start. This becomes the ergonomic primary input channel for coarse section-level timing.
tasks.details:
Extend `pipeline/lyrics_aligner.py` parser in `_split_user_lyrics`:

1. Recognise a new header line form. Canonical examples (support all so users can paste whatever is natural):
   * `[Verse 1 0:12]`
   * `[Chorus 1:00]`
   * `[Pre-chorus 0:42.5]`
   * `[0:12 Verse 1]` (time before label)
   * `[Outro 2:55]`
   Accept either a `[m:ss]` / `[m:ss.mmm]` / `[h:mm:ss]` token inside the brackets (order-insensitive relative to the label). The header is matched on the ENTIRE trimmed line; lines that start with `[` but don't match the header grammar fall through to `_parse_anchor_prefix` (legacy bare-anchor lines) unchanged.
2. On match: emit both a section break (append to `section_starts`) AND record the timestamp in `line_anchors` keyed to the NEXT real lyric line emitted after the header. Collapse consecutive headers the same way consecutive `---` markers are collapsed.
3. Preserve the label text on the output so downstream logging and the editor can surface it (e.g. the Section-fingerprint log line can print `chosen=[Verse 1: 12.00s, Chorus: 42.50s, ...]`). This requires a small widening of the tuple returned by `_split_user_lyrics` or a sibling dict `section_labels: dict[int, str]` keyed by section index.
4. Backward compatibility: `---`, `--- <tag>`, bare `[m:ss]` prefixes on lyric lines, and bare `[m:ss]` on a standalone line must continue to work with identical semantics. Cache hashes for existing songs that use only legacy forms must not change (fold labels into `_lyrics_cache_key` canonical form only when labels/header-timestamps are actually present).
5. Wire the header timestamps into the existing `forced_sec` dict that feeds `_assign_sections_via_temporal_dp` — they should behave identically to today's inline `[m:ss]` anchors (score 2.0, dominates fingerprint match scores capped at 1.0).
6. Optional polish: surface `section_labels` in the persisted `lyrics.aligned.json` payload so the visual editor can display section badges on the waveform.
tasks.testStrategy:
Unit tests in `tests/test_lyrics_aligner.py`:
  * `TestSplitUserLyricsTimestampedHeaders` — parse each canonical header form; verify section_starts, line_anchors, and section_labels are populated; verify labels don't leak into lyric text; verify mixed documents with both `---` and timestamped headers work.
  * Backward-compat: re-run `TestSplitUserLyrics*` existing cases and assert outputs unchanged.
  * Cache-key stability: `_lyrics_cache_key` for a document using only `---` must hash identically before/after this change.
  * `TestSectionFingerprintEndToEnd` — add a case where the user provides headers on every section and verify the DP picks the header times verbatim (score 2.0 wins).
End-to-end: load one real song with 5-8 labeled section headers and confirm the `Section-fingerprint placement:` log line shows every section `chosen_starts[i]` equal to the header time (within 5 ms).
tasks.expansionPrompt: Split into: 1) Enhance lyrics_aligner parser for timestamped header grammar + section_labels dict; 2) Ensure backward compatibility, cache key stability for legacy formats; 3) Integrate header timestamps into forced_sec/DP scoring + optional JSON persistence.

tasks.title: Logo rim lights: line mask, silhouette, and fallbacks
tasks.description: Add preprocessing utilities to derive a stroke/line energy mask (high-luma + optional edge emphasis), a full alpha silhouette, logo centroid, and a confidence score. When line energy is too weak, expose a clear fallback to outer-halo-only behavior for generic logos.
tasks.details: Implement in `pipeline/logo_composite.py` or a small sibling module (e.g. `pipeline/logo_rim_lights.py`). Steps: (1) Convert logo RGBA to linear-ish gray or max(R,G,B) for luma; threshold + optional Scharr/Sobel magnitude; multiply by alpha. (2) Centroid and bounding geometry from alpha for polar coordinates. (3) `line_mask_strength` scalar; if below epsilon, set `use_line_features=False` for later stages. (4) Optional light morphology (dilate/erode) to stabilize thin strokes. (5) Keep APIs pure NumPy, documented. PRD: `.taskmaster/docs/prd-logo-rim-lights.txt`.
tasks.testStrategy: Unit tests with tiny synthetic images: white strokes on black disc (high line confidence); solid blob (low line confidence, fallback path); assert centroid and masks shapes; deterministic across platforms.
tasks.expansionPrompt: Split into: 1) Line mask, silhouette, centroid computation utilities; 2) Confidence scoring + fallback logic + unit tests.

tasks.title: Logo rim lights: traveling wave, radial field, and inward mix
tasks.description: Build the core rim render: phase angle θ(t,ω) around the logo centroid, radial falloff, and smooth blending of outer halo vs line-local energy. Support configurable inward bleed using distance-to-edge or distance transform so light can read as moving into white strokes when enabled.
tasks.details: Depends on task 24 outputs. For each frame: compute angular position of each pixel in logo patch coords; modulate emissive intensity with smooth periodic functions of θ + global phase(t). Combine outer ring (alpha dilated minus core) with line mask contribution. Inward: weight by DT from alpha edge or luma. Use soft ramps (smoothstep) for all transitions. Output premultiplied RGBA patch for `_blend_premult_rgba_patch` (same stack as current glow). Reuse padding/blur where helpful but allow higher-quality multi-pass given hardware budget. Disable tqdm-style noise; keep per-frame work predictable.
tasks.testStrategy: Visual sanity on synthetic stroke logo; assert phase(0) and phase(T) differ predictably; inward mix 0 vs 1 measurably changes mean intensity on stroke pixels; no NaNs.
tasks.expansionPrompt: Split into: 1) Polar coordinate transform + phase computation; 2) Radial falloff + line energy blending; 3) Distance transform inward weighting + premult output.

tasks.title: Logo rim lights: multi-color layers and smooth color motion
tasks.description: Layer 2–3 rim contributions with distinct hues, phase offsets, and optional hue rotation over time. Ensure smooth cross-blends and no banding; tune for neon aesthetic (saturation, optional slight flicker).
tasks.details: Depends on 25. Implement as weighted sum of colored emissive layers in linear light where practical, or gamma-aware blend. Parameters: `color_spread`, `layer_phase_offsets`, `hue_drift_per_sec`, optional per-preset default colors from shadow/base hex. Seeded variety from `song_hash` for repeatability. When `use_line_features` is false, reduce to simpler dual-tone halo that still animates. Document recommended defaults in code comments.
tasks.testStrategy: Determinism: same song_hash + t yields identical frames; at least one test checks hue drift monotonicity over t; no visible discontinuities in a short synthetic sequence (assert max delta in patch under threshold).
tasks.expansionPrompt: Split into: 1) Multi-layer colored emissive blending; 2) Hue rotation/drift + seeded variety + fallback halos.

tasks.title: Logo rim lights: beat, snare, and bass modulation
tasks.description: Modulate global intensity, phase velocity, and inward-mix with existing audio tracks: at minimum snare-glow and bass pulse envelopes from `pipeline/beat_pulse.py` and compositor timing (absolute t).
tasks.details: Extend compositor path so per-frame `t_sec` and prebuilt tracks (or values) can scale: `glow_strength` analog, `rim_phase_mul`, `rim_inward_mul`. Snare hit → brief intensity + optional phase kick; smooth with short attack/release to avoid stutter. Preserve behavior when snare/bass features disabled. Align with `OrchestratorInputs` fields already on `CompositorConfig`.
tasks.testStrategy: Mock tracks: constant, spike, and sine; assert modulation stays in declared ranges; render one frame with snare=0 vs 1 for measurable difference. Regression: existing beat_pulse tests still pass.

tasks.title: Generalized band pulse tracks (lo / mid / hi)

These produce `PulseTrack` envelopes for kick/sub, snare/mids, and hats/sparkle respectively.
tasks.details: Reuse the existing shape (positive diff of averaged bands → one-pole decay → percentile norm). Defaults: lo = bands [0,2) decay 0.34s; mid = bands [3,6) decay 0.12s; hi = bands [6,8) decay 0.06s; all 90th-percentile norm. Keep `build_bass_pulse_track` (used by logo) as a thin wrapper around `build_band_pulse_track`. Export new names from `__all__`. Covered by direct unit tests similar to `test_beat_pulse.py`.

tasks.description: Add a time-domain sampler for the existing `onsets.strength` array in `analysis.json` so shaders get a continuous transient envelope alongside the discrete `onset_pulse`.
tasks.details: Implement `_interp_onset_strength(analysis, t) -> float` in `pipeline/reactive_shader.py` (or a private helper next to `_interp_scalar_series`). The onsets block carries `frame_rate_hz` (not `fps`), so accept that key too. Normalise once (per-load) by the 95th percentile so the output is roughly [0,1] without a single outlier crushing everything. Cache the normalised array on the analysis dict under `analysis['_onset_env_cache']` keyed by id, or recompute cheaply each call — document the choice.

tasks.title: Drop + build-up detector (`pipeline/musical_events.py`)
tasks.description: New module that detects drop events and a build-up tension envelope from RMS + 8-band mel spectrum + segment boundaries already in `analysis.json`. Persist the output under a new `events` key so every render samples the same deterministic events.
tasks.details: Algorithm (deterministic, no ML): (1) smooth RMS ~0.3s; (2) take positive RMS step over a ~0.5s lag window; (3) multiply by low-band energy step over the same lag (to gate mids-only false positives); (4) peak-pick above percentile + absolute floor; (5) snap to the nearest segment boundary within ±1.0s OR keep if the local jump is very strong; (6) de-dupe within a 5s window. For `build_tension(t)`: for each drop `t_d` with confidence `c`, rise linearly (or smoothstep) over [t_d - T_build, t_d] to `c`, snap back to 0 just after `t_d`; take max across overlapping build windows. Persist: `events = { 'drops': [{'t', 'confidence'}...], 'build_tension': {'fps': int, 'values': [float]}, 'build_window_sec': float }`. Call from `analyze_song` right after segments, before writing JSON.
tasks.testStrategy: Unit: synthetic RMS with a flat section followed by a step produces exactly one drop near the step; build_tension rises to `confidence` at `t_d` and collapses to 0 immediately after. Degenerate inputs (no spectrum, no segments) return empty events without raising.
tasks.expansionPrompt: Split into: 1) detect_drops() + segment snapping + de-dup; 2) build_tension per-frame series + drop_hold sampler + JSON serialisation.

tasks.title: Bar-phase helper (downbeat-aware)
tasks.description: Add a `bar_phase_at(t, downbeats, beats_per_bar=4, bpm=None) -> float` helper that returns the [0,1) position within a 4-beat bar based on BeatNet downbeats, falling back to beat-grid + bpm when downbeats are absent.
tasks.details: When downbeats exist, find the most recent downbeat `d_prev` and next `d_next`; return `(t - d_prev) / (d_next - d_prev)`. When only beats exist, group beats into bars of length `beats_per_bar` and return phase within the bar. Outside the grid, fall back to `beat_phase_at` behaviour extrapolated with `60/bpm * beats_per_bar` as the bar period. Lives in `pipeline/reactive_shader.py` near `_beat_phase_at`.
tasks.testStrategy: Unit: downbeats at [0, 2, 4, 6] with `t=1.0` returns 0.5; `t=5.5` returns 0.75; empty downbeats + 120 bpm gives phase period 2.0s.

tasks.title: Feedback / warp framebuffer in `ReactiveShader`

Reset state on re-use so the preview starts clean. Document VRAM cost (~16 MB at 1080p RGBA8).

tasks.title: Implement chromatic_aberration renderer
tasks.description: Create chromatic_aberration.py for full-frame per-channel RGB shift driven by active CHROMATIC_ABERRATION clips
tasks.details:
pipeline/chromatic_aberration.py:
def apply_chromatic_aberration(frame: np.ndarray, t: float, clips: list[EffectClip], song_hash: str) -> np.ndarray:
  # Find active CHROMATIC_ABERRATION clips at t; early-return frame untouched if none.
  # Per-clip settings: shift_px (float, max pixel offset of R/B channels), jitter (0..1, seeded per-frame wobble), direction_deg (optional fixed angle; default random-per-clip seeded from song_hash + clip.id).
  # Per-frame seed: hash(song_hash + clip.id + round(t*1000)) -> reproducible jitter matching glitch_seed_for_time pattern.
  # Implementation: split RGB -> np.roll / cv2.warpAffine by (dx, dy) computed from (shift_px, angle + jitter), recombine. G channel stays fixed; R and B shift opposite directions so the aberration reads.
  # Clamp shift so it never exceeds min(H, W) * 0.1 to keep output readable.
  # Return new ndarray, never mutate input in-place (keeps the compositor frame-queue invariant intact).
Keep it pure NumPy (matches the pipeline's dependency floor); fall back to skimage/cv2 only if profiling shows a hot-path win.
tasks.testStrategy: Frame bit-identical to input when no clip active at t. With one active clip at strength>0: R and B channels shifted by expected pixel count, G channel unchanged, output dtype/shape preserved. Deterministic under fixed song_hash + clip.id + t. Clip with shift_px=0 returns input frame unchanged.
tasks.expansionPrompt: Break into: 1) Core logic for finding active CHROMATIC_ABERRATION clips at time t, compute per-frame deterministic shifts using song_hash+clip.id+t seed, handle jitter/direction_deg settings with bounds; 2) NumPy implementation of RGB channel splitting, np.roll/warpAffine shifts (R/B opposite directions, G fixed), recombination returning new ndarray without mutation.

tasks.title: Implement scanline_tear renderer
tasks.description: Create scanline_tear.py for horizontal row-offset distortion driven by active SCANLINE_TEAR clips
tasks.details:
pipeline/scanline_tear.py:
def apply_scanline_tear(frame: np.ndarray, t: float, clips: list[EffectClip], song_hash: str) -> np.ndarray:
  # Find active SCANLINE_TEAR clips at t; early-return untouched if none.
  # Per-clip settings: intensity (0..1, max horizontal offset as fraction of W), band_count (int, number of torn horizontal bands, default 3-6), band_height_px (optional), wrap_mode ('wrap' | 'clamp' | 'black', default 'wrap').
  # Per-frame seed: hash(song_hash + clip.id + round(t*1000)). Pick band y-positions + per-band dx from RNG; apply np.roll along axis=1 per band.
  # Accumulate contributions across overlapping clips (later clips stack on earlier). Return new ndarray.
  # Keep shifts bounded to +/- intensity * W * 0.25 so tears never displace more than a quarter of the frame width.
Reference pattern: pipeline/logo_rim_beams.py deterministic seeding; pipeline/chromatic_aberration.py for the ndarray-in-ndarray-out contract.
tasks.testStrategy: Frame bit-identical to input when no clip active. With one clip at intensity>0: exactly `band_count` bands shifted horizontally, G/R/B all shifted together (no channel split), output dtype/shape preserved. Deterministic under fixed song_hash + clip.id + t. intensity=0 returns input frame unchanged.
tasks.expansionPrompt: Split into: 1) Clip activation detection and per-frame deterministic band positioning/offsets using hash(song_hash + clip.id + round(t*1000)), handle intensity/band_count/band_height_px/wrap_mode settings; 2) Multi-band horizontal shifting via np.roll per band with accumulation across overlapping clips, bounded shifts, returning new ndarray.

tasks.title: Unify analyser-timed FX under the effects timeline (single control surface)
tasks.description: Remove duplicate controls outside the Effects timeline editor for glitch, beams, and other kinds that are currently driven both from main Gradio (e.g. branding/impact) and from the timeline/ghost/bake path. The timeline becomes the only user-facing place to enable and shape those events.
tasks.details:
Product model:
- Default per-row `auto_enabled` in the effects editor to OFF for kinds that today stack invisible compositor auto on top of clips (at minimum LOGO_GLITCH and BEAM).
- When the user turns auto ON for a row, materialize `EffectClip` rows from the same analyser sources as ghosts/bake (`build_ghost_events` / `bake_auto_schedule` logic)—no parallel compositor auto that adds the same hits again.
- Clips on the timeline are authoritative: edit, shorten, or delete a clip and render matches.

Implementation direction:
1) LOGO_GLITCH: Stop surfacing standalone logo impact glitch controls in Branding (or repoint them into timeline-only semantics). Compositor must not sum `_impact_envelope_fn` with user clips when the timeline owns glitch—clips-only path. Decide fate of `logo_impact_glitch_strength` / sensitivity (fixed internal defaults, migrate to clip settings, or per-timeline JSON fields).
2) BEAM: When timeline is authoritative, skip analyser `schedule_rim_beams` auto leg so baked/manual BEAM clips are not duplicated; auto ON generates beam clips from `_schedule_beams_for_ghosts` / same schedule as today.
3) Audit ZOOM/SCREEN_SHAKE/CHROMATIC (already clip-first for render), SCANLINE_TEAR/COLOR_INVERT (ghost/bake gaps), and align defaults + docs.
4) UX: Clarify auto checkbox + optional explicit “generate clips” flow; update `docs/technical/effects-timeline-editor.md`, `effects-timeline-compositor.md`, `effects-timeline-gradio-tab.md`; extend `tests/test_compositor_effects_timeline.py` and effects editor tests.

Migration: existing `effects_timeline.json` files may need default `auto_enabled` false for affected kinds; document behaviour for old projects that relied on Branding sliders alone.
tasks.testStrategy: No double application of glitch or beam auto vs clips; with fresh timeline defaults and no clips, hidden analyser glitch/beams do not fire until auto generates clips or user adds clips; pytest compositor + effects editor coverage; manual smoke on Effects timeline tab + preview render.
tasks.expansionPrompt: Divide into: 1) Compositor flags / timeline schema for clip-authoritative BEAM + LOGO_GLITCH and removal of duplicate auto paths; 2) Gradio/app.py remove or relocate Branding controls; effects_editor defaults + auto-on clip generation wiring; 3) SCANLINE/COLOR_INVERT and remaining kinds audit; 4) Docs + migration note + tests.
